title: CRC32 fallback lets unsigned frames pass peer-authenticated RX path
description:
Trigger: with CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_INSECURE_CRC32_MIC enabled and no link key loaded, lichen_l2_input() passes rx_ctx.link_key=NULL to lichen_link_rx() at firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:1815-1848. peer_try_all_pubkeys() then calls lichen_link_rx() once per known peer at firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:543-560, but lichen_link_rx() only verifies Schnorr when parsed.signature_present is set at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_rx.c:181-215. For an unsigned CRC32 frame, it still assigns src_eui64 from ctx->peer_eui64 at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_rx.c:234-237, skips replay allocation because parsed.signature_present is false at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_rx.c:282-291, and accepts the packet after CRC32 verification/decompression.

Impact: the bridge comments claim frames are accepted only when a known peer pubkey verifies the Schnorr signature (firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:1831-1843), but an attacker can forge CRC32 and inject unsigned IPv6 packets that are attributed to the first active peer. The Kconfig labels CRC32 insecure at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/Kconfig:56-70, so this should either be blocked in the peer-authenticated L2 path or clearly separated from authenticated RX semantics.

Relevant refs: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:543, firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:1815, firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:1831, lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_rx.c:181, lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_rx.c:234, lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_rx.c:290, lichen/subsys/lichen/link/Kconfig:56.
close_reason: Fixed: peer_try_all_pubkeys now rejects frames without Schnorr signatures before iterating known peer pubkeys, keeping CRC32 fallback out of the peer-authenticated bridge RX path. git diff --check passed; bridge Zephyr builds are blocked by existing CMake issue project-LICHEN-i81y.19.
